summary: A single Jest test assertion failed in `test/config/get.ts`. The test `pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc` expected the registry URL `https://my-org.registry.example.com` (without trailing slash) but received `https://my-org.registry.example.com/` (with trailing slash), causing the entire CI job to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The test at test/config/get.ts:32 asserts that `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` returns `https://my-org.registry.example.com` (no trailing slash), but the actual output is `https://my-org.registry.example.com/` (with a trailing slash). This is a behavior mismatch — pnpm is now normalizing/appending a trailing slash to scoped registry URLs when reading them back via `config get`.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: Identifies the specific test file that failed.

evidence.quote: ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Identifies the specific test case that failed.
Shows the exact assertion mismatch: expected no trailing slash, received with trailing slash.
Pinpoints the exact line in the test file where the assertion failed.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly 1 test suite and 1 test failed, consistent with the single identified failure.
Confirms the jest run exited with a non-zero code, causing the CI job to fail.

suggested_fix: Update the test assertion at test/config/get.ts:32 to expect `https://my-org.registry.example.com/` (with trailing slash), or fix the `pnpm config get` implementation to strip the trailing slash from scoped registry URLs before returning them, depending on which behavior is intended.
